steps.title: Connect the simile to the claim
steps.content: Bartley claims that any misfortune coming into the world lands on him. The underlined simile, like a flock of crows on seed potatoes, gives that complaint a vivid image of something descending quickly and heavily.

steps.title: Identify the function
steps.content: Choice B treats the image as a literal fair event, Choice C says it casts doubt, and Choice D shifts the focus to Mrs. Tarpey. The underlined portion is figurative language that emphasizes Bartley's own claim. The answer is A.
